name: Rice, Lentil and Spinach Pilaf
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Fresh spinach, well washed; cut into 1/2-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the lentils and place them in a saucepan. Cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover the pan.
2. Simmer for 25 to 40 minutes, or until tender but still al dente. Drain.
3. In a separate pot, bring rice and 2 cups of water to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer covered for about 20 minutes, until all liquid is absorbed. Set aside.
4. Bring vegetable stock to a boil in a saucepan. Add onions and simmer until tender and translucent, about 10 minutes.
5. Add garlic, celery, cumin, and cinnamon. Simmer for 5 minutes longer.
6. Add lentils and lemon zest (and optional tomatoes) to the pan. Heat through.
7. Stir in spinach. When greens are tender, fold into rice mixture.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
Note: This can be made ahead of time and placed in a baking pan, covered with foil, then warmed in the oven for 15-20 minutes. Cooked cubes of carrots or butternut squash may also be added to this mixture.
